USAGE: eqx [--help] [--quiet] [--verbose] [--verboseconsole] [--localseq]
           [--logfile <string>] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    dump <options>        Load and show events in a specified stream (supports
                          all stores).
    loadtest <options>    Run a load test
    init <options>        Initialize Store/Container (supports `cosmos` stores;
                          also handles RU/s provisioning adjustment).
    initaws <options>     Initialize DynamoDB Table (supports `dynamo` stores;
                          also handles RU/s provisioning adjustment).
    initsql <options>     Initialize Database Schema (supports
                          `mssql`/`mysql`/`postgres` SqlStreamStore stores).
    stats <options>       inspect store to determine numbers of
                          streams/documents/events and/or config (supports
                          `cosmos` and `dynamo` stores).
    query <options>       Load/Summarise streams based on Cosmos SQL Queries
                          (supports `cosmos` only).
    top <options>         Scan to determine top categories and streams
                          (supports `cosmos` only).
    destroy <options>     DELETE documents for a nominated category and/or
                          stream (includes a dry-run mode). (supports `cosmos`
                          only).

    Use 'eqx <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --quiet, -Q           Omit timestamps from log output
    --verbose, -V         Include low level logging regarding specific test
                          runs.
    --verboseconsole, -C  Include low level test and store actions logging in
                          on-screen output to console.
    --localseq, -S        Configures writing to a local Seq endpoint at
                          http://localhost:5341, see https://getseq.net
    --logfile, -l <string>
                          specify a log file to write the result breakdown into
                          (default: eqx.log).
    --help                display this list of options.

USAGE: eqx destroy [--help] [--streamname <string>] [--streamlike <string>]
                   [--categoryname <string>] [--categorylike <string>]
                   [--customfilter <sql>] [--force] [--parallelism <dop>]
                   <subcommand> [<options>]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    cosmos <options>      Parameters for CosmosDB.

    Use 'eqx <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --streamname, -sn <string>
                          Specify stream name to match against `p`, e.g.
                          `$UserServices-f7c1ce63389a45bdbea1cccebb1b3c8a`.
    --streamlike, -sl <string>
                          Specify stream name to match against `p`, e.g.
                          `%-f7c1ce63389a45bdbea1cccebb1b3c8a`.
    --categoryname, -cn <string>
                          Specify category name to match against `p`, e.g.
                          `$UserServices`.
    --categorylike, -cl <string>
                          Specify category name to match against `p` as a
                          Cosmos LIKE expression (with `%` as wildcard, e.g.
                          `$UserServices-%`).
    --customfilter, -cs <sql>
                          Specify a custom filter, referencing the document as
                          `c.` (e.g. `'c.p LIKE "test-%" AND c._ts <
                          1717138092'`)
    --force, -f           Actually delete the documents (default is a dry run,
                          reporting what would be deleted)
    --parallelism, -w <dop>
                          Number of concurrent delete requests permitted to run
                          in parallel. Default: 32
    --help                display this list of options.

USAGE: eqx destroy cosmos [--help] [--storeverbose]
                          [--connectionmode <gateway|direct>]
                          [--timeout <double>] [--retries <int>]
                          [--retrieswaittimes <double>] [--connection <string>]
                          [--database <string>] [--container <string>]
                          [--archiveconnection <string>]
                          [--archivedatabase <string>]
                          [--archivecontainer <string>] [--tipmaxevents <int>]
                          [--tipmaxjsonlength <int>] [--querymaxitems <int>]

OPTIONS:

    --storeverbose, -V    Include low level Store logging.
    --connectionmode, -m <gateway|direct>
                          override the connection mode. Default: Direct.
    --timeout, -o <double>
                          specify operation timeout in seconds (default: 5).
    --retries, -r <int>   specify operation retries (default: 1).
    --retrieswaittimes, -rt <double>
                          specify max wait-time for retry when being throttled
                          by Cosmos in seconds (default: 5)
    --connection, -s <string>
                          specify a connection string for a Cosmos account.
                          (optional if environment variable
                          EQUINOX_COSMOS_CONNECTION specified)
    --database, -d <string>
                          specify a database name for store. (optional if
                          environment variable EQUINOX_COSMOS_DATABASE
                          specified)
    --container, -c <string>
                          specify a container name for store. (optional if
                          environment variable EQUINOX_COSMOS_CONTAINER
                          specified)
    --archiveconnection, -s2 <string>
                          specify a connection string for Archive Cosmos
                          account. Default: use same as Primary Connection
    --archivedatabase, -d2 <string>
                          specify a database name for Archive store. Default:
                          use same as Primary Database
    --archivecontainer, -c2 <string>
                          specify a container name for Archive store. Default:
                          use same as Primary Container
    --tipmaxevents, -te <int>
                          specify maximum number of events to hold in Tip
                          before calving off to a frozen Batch. Default: 256
    --tipmaxjsonlength, -tl <int>
                          specify maximum length of JSON (as measured by
                          JSON.stringify) to hold in Tip before calving off to
                          a frozen Batch. Default: 30,000
    --querymaxitems, -b <int>
                          specify maximum number of batches of events to
                          retrieve in per query response. Default: 10/9999
    --help                display this list of options.

USAGE: eqx dump [--help] [--streamlike <string>] [--correlation] [--blobs]
                [--jsonskip] [--pretty] [--flattenunfolds] [--timeregular]
                [--intervals] [--names] [--unfoldsonly] [--eventsonly]
                <subcommand> [<options>] [<string>]

STREAM:

    <string>              Specify stream(s) to dump.

SUBCOMMANDS:

    cosmos <options>      Parameters for CosmosDB.
    dynamo <options>      Parameters for DynamoDB.
    es <options>          Parameters for EventStore.
    mdb <options>         Parameters for MessageDB.
    mssql, ms <options>   Parameters for Sql Server.
    mysql, my <options>   Parameters for MySql.
    postgres, pg <options>
                          Parameters for Postgres.

    Use 'eqx <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --streamlike, -sl <string>
                          (CosmosDB only) Specify stream name pattern to dump:
                          LIKE expression with `%` and `_` tokens etc.
    --correlation, -C     Include Correlation/Causation identifiers
    --blobs, -B           Don't assume Data/Metadata is UTF-8 text
    --jsonskip, -J        Don't assume Data/Metadata is JSON
    --pretty, -P          Pretty print the JSON over multiple lines
    --flattenunfolds, -F  Don't pretty print the JSON over multiple lines for
                          Unfolds. Quiet mode: Pretty print
    --timeregular, -T     Don't humanize time intervals between events
    --intervals, -I       Omit intervals between events. Quiet mode: Include
                          intervals
    --names, -N           Emit StreamName prior to events/unfolds instead of
                          adding log context. Quiet mode: exclude stream names
    --unfoldsonly, -U     Exclude Events. Default: show both Events and Unfolds
    --eventsonly, -E      Exclude Unfolds/Snapshots. Default: show both Events
                          and Unfolds.
    --help                display this list of options.

USAGE: eqx dump dynamo [--help] [--storeverbose] [--regionprofile <string>]
                       [--serviceurl <string>] [--accesskey <string>]
                       [--secretkey <string>] [--table <string>]
                       [--archivetable <string>] [--retries <int>]
                       [--retriestimeouts <double>] [--tipmaxbytes <int>]
                       [--tipmaxevents <int>] [--querymaxitems <int>]

OPTIONS:

    --storeverbose, -V    Include low level Store logging.
    --regionprofile, -sr <string>
                          specify an AWS Region (aka System Name, e.g.
                          "us-east-1") to connect to using the implicit AWS
                          SDK/tooling config and/or environment variables etc.
                          Optional if:
                          1) $EQUINOX_DYNAMO_REGION specified OR
                          2) Explicit
                          `ServiceUrl`/$EQUINOX_DYNAMO_SERVICE_URL+`AccessKey`/$
                          EQUINOX_DYNAMO_ACCESS_KEY_ID+`Secret
                          Key`/$EQUINOX_DYNAMO_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY specified.
                          See
                          https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-c
                          onfigure-envvars.html for details
    --serviceurl, -su <string>
                          specify a server endpoint for a Dynamo account. (Not
                          applicable if `ServiceRegion`/$EQUINOX_DYNAMO_REGION
                          specified; Optional if $EQUINOX_DYNAMO_SERVICE_URL
                          specified)
    --accesskey, -sa <string>
                          specify an access key id for a Dynamo account. (Not
                          applicable if `ServiceRegion`/$EQUINOX_DYNAMO_REGION
                          specified; Optional if $EQUINOX_DYNAMO_ACCESS_KEY_ID
                          specified)
    --secretkey, -ss <string>
                          specify a secret access key for a Dynamo account.
                          (Not applicable if
                          `ServiceRegion`/$EQUINOX_DYNAMO_REGION specified;
                          Optional if $EQUINOX_DYNAMO_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
                          specified)
    --table, -t <string>  specify a table name for the primary store. (optional
                          if $EQUINOX_DYNAMO_TABLE specified)
    --archivetable, -ta <string>
                          specify a table name for the Archive; Optional if
                          $EQUINOX_DYNAMO_TABLE_ARCHIVE specified.
                          Default: Do not attempt to look in an Archive store
                          as a Fallback to locate pruned events.
    --retries, -r <int>   specify operation retries (default: 1).
    --retriestimeouts, -rt <double>
                          specify max wait-time including retries in seconds
                          (default: 5)
    --tipmaxbytes, -tb <int>
                          specify maximum number of bytes to hold in Tip before
                          calving off to a frozen Batch. Default: 32K
    --tipmaxevents, -te <int>
                          specify maximum number of events to hold in Tip
                          before calving off to a frozen Batch. Default:
                          limited by Max Bytes
    --querymaxitems, -b <int>
                          specify maximum number of batches of events to
                          retrieve in per query response. Default: 10
    --help                display this list of options.

                 <subcommand> [<options>]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    cosmos <options>      Parameters for CosmosDB.

    Use 'eqx <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --streamname, -sn <string>
                          Specify stream name to match against `p`, e.g.
                          `$UserServices-f7c1ce63389a45bdbea1cccebb1b3c8a`.
    --streamlike, -sl <string>
                          Specify stream name to match against `p`, e.g.
                          `%-f7c1ce63389a45bdbea1cccebb1b3c8a`.
    --categoryname, -cn <string>
                          Specify category name to match against `p`, e.g.
                          `$UserServices`.
    --categorylike, -cl <string>
                          Specify category name to match against `p` as a
                          Cosmos LIKE expression (with `%` as wildcard, e.g.
                          `$UserServices-%`).
    --unfoldname, -un <string>
                          Specify unfold Name to match against `u.c`, e.g.
                          `Snapshotted`
    --unfoldcriteria, -uc <string>
                          Specify constraints on Unfold (reference unfold
                          fields via `u.d.`, top level fields via `c.`), e.g.
                          `u.d.name = "TenantName1"`.
    --mode, -m <snaponly|snapwithstream|default|readonly|readwithstream|raw>
                          snaponly: Only read `u[0].d+D`
                          snapwithstream: As per `snaponly`, but add `p`
                          (stream name)`.
                          default: As per `snapwithstream`, but add `_etag`
                          readonly: Only read `u`nfolds, not `_etag`.
                          readwithstream: Read `u`nfolds and `p` (stream name),
                          but not `_etag`.
                          raw: Read Items (documents) in full. <- Default when
                          Output File specified
    --file, -o <string>   Export full retrieved JSON to file. NOTE this
                          switches the default mode to `Raw`
    --pretty, -P          Render the JSON indented over multiple lines
    --console, -C         Also emit the JSON to the console. Default: Gather
                          statistics (but only write to a File if specified)
    --help                display this list of options.
